options.description: System module name (default: System.Private.CoreLib) [default: System.Private.CoreLib]

options.description: Name of the module from which to copy Win32 resources (Windows target only)

options.name: --multifile
options.description: Compile only input files (do not compile referenced assemblies)

options.name: --waitfordebugger
options.description: Pause to give opportunity to attach debugger

options.name: --completetypemetadata

options.description: Reflection data to generate (one of: all, none)

options.description: Scan IL for reflection patterns

options.description: Use IL scanner to generate optimized code (implied by -O)

options.description: Do not use IL scanner to generate optimized code

options.description: Do not generate inline thread local statics

options.description: Emit data to support generating stack trace strings at runtime

options.name: --methodbodyfolding
options.description: Fold identical method bodies

options.description: Feature switches to apply (format: 'Namespace.Name=[true|false]' []

options.description: Runtime knobs to set []

options.name: --parallelism
options.description: Maximum number of threads to use during compilation [default: 4]

options.description: Instruction set to allow or disallow

options.name: --max-vectort-bitwidth
options.description: Maximum width, in bits, that Vector<T> is allowed to be

options.description: Enable mitigations. Options: 'cf': CFG (Control Flow Guard, Windows only)

options.name: --dehydrate
options.description: Dehydrate runtime data structures

options.name: --preinitstatics
options.description: Interpret static constructors at compile time if possible (implied by -O)

options.name: --nopreinitstatics
options.description: Do not interpret static constructors at compile time

options.description: Disable specific warning messages []

options.name: --singlewarn
options.description: Generate single AOT/trimming warning per assembly

options.name: --notrimwarn
options.description: Disable warnings related to trimming

options.name: --noaotwarn
options.description: Disable warnings related to AOT

options.name: --singlewarnassembly
options.description: Generate single AOT/trimming warning for given assembly []

options.name: --nosinglewarnassembly
options.description: Expand AOT/trimming warnings for given assembly []

options.name: --warnaserror
options.description: Treat warnings as errors

options.name: --warnaserr
options.description: Enable treating specific warnings as errors

options.name: --nowarnaserr
options.description: Disable treating specific warnings as errors

options.name: --directpinvoke
options.description: PInvoke to call directly []

options.name: --directpinvokelist
options.description: File with list of PInvokes to call directly []

options.name: --maxgenericcycle
options.description: Max depth of generic cycle [default: 4]

options.name: --maxgenericcyclebreadth
options.description: Max breadth of generic cycle expansion [default: 10]

options.name: --root
options.description: Fully generate given assembly []

options.name: --conditionalroot
options.description: Fully generate given assembly if it's used []

options.name: --trim

options.name: --defaultrooting
options.description: Root assemblies that are not marked [IsTrimmable]

options.name: --targetarch
options.description: Target architecture for cross compilation [default: X64]

options.name: --targetos
options.description: Target OS for cross compilation [default: Linux]

options.name: --jitpath
options.description: Path to JIT compiler library

options.name: --singlemethodtypename
options.description: Single method compilation: assembly-qualified name of the owning type

options.name: --singlemethodname
options.description: Single method compilation: name of the method

options.name: --singlemethodgenericarg
options.description: Single method compilation: generic arguments to the method

options.name: --make-repro-path
options.description: Path where to place a repro package

options.name: --generateunmanagedentrypoints
options.description:
Generate unmanaged entrypoints for a given assembly []
Options may be passed on the command line, or via response file. On the command line switch values may be specified by passing the option followed by a space followed by the value of the option, or by specifying a : between option and switch value. A response file is specified by passing the @ symbol before the response file name. In a response file all options must be specified on their own lines, and only the : syntax for switches is supported.
Use the '--' option to disambiguate between input files that have begin with -- and options. After a '--' option, all arguments are considered to be input files. If no input files begin with '--' then this option is not necessary.
Valid switches for --targetos are: 'windows', 'linux', 'freebsd', 'osx', 'maccatalyst', 'ios', 'iossimulator', 'tvos', 'tvossimulator'. The default value is 'linux'
Valid switches for --targetarch are: 'arm', 'arm64', 'x86', 'x64', 'riscv64'. The default value is 'x64'
The allowable values for the --instruction-set option are described in the table below. Each architecture has a different set of valid instruction sets, and multiple instruction sets may be specified by separating the instructions sets by a ','. For example 'avx2,bmi,lzcnt'
arm64: base, neon, aes, crc, dotprod, rdma, sha1, sha2, lse, rcpc, vectort128, rcpc2, sve
x86: base, sse, sse2, sse3, ssse3, sse4.1, sse4.2, avx, avx2, aes, bmi, bmi2, fma, lzcnt, pclmul, popcnt, avxvnni, movbe, serialize, evex, avx512f, avx512f_vl, avx512bw, avx512bw_vl, avx512cd, avx512cd_vl, avx512dq, avx512dq_vl, avx512vbmi, avx512vbmi_vl, avx10v1, avx10v1_v512, vectort128, vectort256, vectort512
x64: base, sse, sse2, sse3, ssse3, sse4.1, sse4.2, avx, avx2, aes, bmi, bmi2, fma, lzcnt, pclmul, popcnt, avxvnni, movbe, serialize, evex, avx512f, avx512f_vl, avx512bw, avx512bw_vl, avx512cd, avx512cd_vl, avx512dq, avx512dq_vl, avx512vbmi, avx512vbmi_vl, avx10v1, avx10v1_v512, vectort128, vectort256, vectort512
The following CPU names are predefined groups of instruction sets and can be used in --instruction-set too:
x86-x64, x86-x64-v2, x86-x64-v3, skylake, x86-x64-v4, armv8-a, armv8.1-a, armv8.2-a, armv8.3-a, armv8.4-a, armv8.5-a, armv8.6-a, apple-m1
